Name: Mia
Message: Good morning! I'm about to load the first batch of linens into the industrial dryers.

Name: Rosa
Message: Hold on Mia, which pile are you loading?

Name: Mia
Message: The mixed linens from the East Wing. I'm setting the dryer to 78°C because that is what the SOP states.

Name: Rosa
Message: Are there any polyester sheets from Hotel A in that pile?

Name: Mia
Message: Yes a few of them. Does that matter?

Name: Rosa
Message: Yes. The 10-year laundry worker knows that polyester from Hotel A always shrinks if you run it on the same cycle as cotton.

Name: David
Message: Wait, Rosa, I thought we ran the Hotel A polyester at 80°C to sanitize it faster?

Name: Rosa
Message: No, David. If you run it at 80°C, it ruins the fabric. You have to run it at 75°C.

Name: David
Message: But the SOP says 78°C. We should probably stick to that.

Name: Rosa
Message: If you run it at 78°C, the corners will bunch up and the client will complain. 75°C is the only way it doesn't shrink.

Name: Mia
Message: So... Worker A says 80°C, Worker B says 75°C, and the SOP says 78°C. Which one do I actually use?

Name: David
Message: I'll escalate this to the facility manager to get the SOP officially updated. For today, follow Rosa's advice and use 75°C.

Name: Mia
Message: Got it. 75°C it is.

Name: Rosa
Message: My back is killing me today. This concrete floor is brutal.

Name: David
Message: Make sure you're wearing the anti-fatigue mats.

Name: Rosa
Message: They don't help. I need a vacation.

Name: Mia
Message: The washing machine on the left is vibrating really loudly.

Name: Rosa
Message: That's normal for machine 2 during the spin cycle. Just ignore it unless it starts beeping.

Name: Mia
Message: The manual says to hit the emergency stop if there is excessive vibration.

Name: David
Message: Rosa is right, machine 2 has a slightly off-center drum. As long as it isn't beeping, it's safe.
